title: Ralph Phase P: session closure — 5 cycles exhausted, document findings
context: Five consecutive Ralph cycles (K→L→M→N→O, all architect-APPROVED) have tested every reachable kernel lever: SDPA compute_kernel_config, attn_sub_norm amplification hypothesis, BITNET_RMSNORM_FP32_ACC default flip (landed), bfp8 KV cache (3x regression), ttnn.split vs slice (neutral), ternary_matmul HiFi4 (false lever: identical PCC, -2.35 t/s), decode-path RoPE LoFi (false lever: -1.11 t/s, output divergence). Every tt-metal API-level kernel lever reachable from bitnet-tt Python has now been tried and either landed, redirected, or falsified. Further wins require editing tt-metal compute kernel C++ source (ternary_mm_compute.cpp algorithmic changes, sdpa.cpp softmax max-reduce variants) which triggers a 30+ min tt-metal rebuild per attempt — out of Ralph session scope.

stories.title: Append Phase O+P closure summary to session_14_optimization_ceiling.md
stories.finding: Doc appended with (1) full commit list through 43aefc4, (2) K–O verdict table, (3) explicit prerequisite for the next cycle (tt-metal C++ edit + rebuild). Commit + push on this iteration.
acceptanceCriteria: Doc has a 'Session closure' section listing all 5 cycles with one-line verdict each
Doc states the explicit next-action prerequisite (tt-metal source edit + rebuild) for any further progress
Doc references the 10 commits in chronological order (949e2ff through 43aefc4)
